mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, triggering inflation expectations that force the Federal Reserve to hike rates faster than equity earnings can grow, compressing valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the threshold relationship breaks down during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion combined with robust industrial production signals synchronized demand-pull growth, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the cross-correlation between RSAFS and INDPRO becomes decoupled from equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates initially support bank profitability and credit supply, boosting equity returns until the unemployment rate begins rising, at which point the signal flips to negative.
failure_condition: The positive return predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5%, or the signal produces negative returns during early-stage rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, leading to sustained increases in durable goods spending and corporate revenues that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or housing starts decline while equity returns remain positive for > 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 98.0, capacity constraints emerge, driving up input costs and compressing corporate margins, which negatively impacts equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when INDPRO stays above 98.0 for > 4 consecutive months, or the threshold relationship reverses during supply-side technological booms.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with elevated inflation signals an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary tightening, ultimately triggering equity drawdowns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5%, or equity markets rally despite simultaneous spikes in PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low rates, strong consumer credit growth sustains equity valuations by offsetting discount rate pressures, but this positive effect disappears once the federal funds rate crosses 2.0%, at which point credit expansion fails to support multiples.
failure_condition: The positive predictive power of RSAFS vanishes when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%, or forward returns turn negative despite rising credit and sub-2% rates.
